commands.review_summary:
Adds a built-in `apple-container` SSH-lease provider with config/env/flags, provider registration, provider docs, and Go regression coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new provider rather than reporting a broken existing behavior. The branch includes partial live lifecycle output, but not a high-confidence full behavior reproduction for SSH/sync/run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human maintainer scope review and contributor-supplied Apple-silicon E2E proof; automation cannot produce proof from the contributor's hardware setup.

target.title: feat: add apple-container local development provider
target.body:
## Summary

- add `provider: apple-container` as a built-in local-development provider for Apple's native `container` runtime (https://github.com/apple/container) on Apple-silicon macOS
- provider-owned adapter under `internal/providers/applecontainer` that shells out to the `container` CLI for lifecycle (`run`/`ls`/`inspect`/`delete`) and runs command exec + file sync over standard Crabbox SSH, reusing the core SSH helpers exactly as `local-container`/`ascii-box` do
- add config/env/flags, provider docs, provider registration, and table-driven regression coverage

## Design Notes

Apple's runtime is **not** assumed Docker-compatible, so this does not reuse the `local-container` Docker code. Each Apple container gets its own routable IP on the host bridge, so the SSH target is the container IP on port 22 (CIDR-stripped from the inspect `networks` address) rather than a published loopback host port. Apple-specific lifecycle stays behind the adapter; core stays provider-neutral.

The CLI surface is grounded in the apple/container README and docs (`container run -d --name --label ... <image>`, `container ls --all`, `container inspect`, `container delete --force`). A few undocumented details (label location, run-id echo) are handled CLI-consistently and noted in code comments.

**Alias-collision avoidance:** the bare `container` alias already belongs to `local-container`. This provider uses `apple-container` with aliases `apple` and `applecontainer` only — asserted by tests in both the provider package and `internal/providers/all`.

The provider is gated to macOS in `Configure`/`Doctor`, rejects non-Linux targets and `--tailscale`, and passes no secrets as CLI arguments (only the SSH public key, via env).

## Config / Secrets

- CLI path env: `CRABBOX_APPLE_CONTAINER_CLI`
- config block: `appleContainer.*` (image, workdir, extra run args) — see `docs/providers/apple-container.md`
- aliases: `apple`, `applecontainer`

## Verification

Local gate on the branch:

```text
gofmt -w $(git ls-files '*.go')                       # clean
go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox        # OK
go vet ./...                                           # OK
go test -race ./internal/cli ./internal/providers/applecontainer ./internal/providers/all   # ok
go test -race ./...                                    # ok
git diff --check                                       # clean
npm run docs:check                                     # ok
```

## Live / E2E

Run against this Apple-silicon Mac (`Darwin arm64`) with `container` CLI v0.3.0 installed and `apiserver running`:

```text
$ crabbox doctor --provider apple-container
ok  provider provider=apple-container timeout=10s cli=ready control_plane=local system=ready inventory=ready mutation=false leases=0 image=ubuntu:26.04

$ crabbox run --provider apple-container --no-sync --keep -- true
provisioning provider=apple-container lease=cbx_8a7e0b0cf223 slug=brisk-barnacle image=ubuntu:26.04 keep=true
waiting for 192.168.64.3:22 apple container ssh... elapsed=1s remaining=19m59s ports=22:closed

$ container ls --all
ID                               IMAGE                           OS     ARCH   STATE    ADDR
crabbox-brisk-barnacle-e8c27c92  docker.io/library/ubuntu:26.04  linux  arm64  stopped

$ crabbox stop --provider apple-container brisk-barnacle
released lease=cbx_8a7e0b0cf223 container=crabbox-brisk-barnacle-e8c27c92

$ container ls --all
ID  IMAGE  OS  ARCH  STATE  ADDR
```

**Proven, observed first-hand:** doctor readiness against the real CLI; `create` provisioning a labeled container with its own routable IP (`192.168.64.3`); SSH-target resolution to the container IP on port 22; and `stop` resolving the lease by slug, deleting the container, and leaving a clean `container ls`.

**Not yet proven (live blocker):** the in-guest SSH bootstrap + sync + command-run step. The stock `ubuntu:26.04` image has no `sshd`, so the SSH wait stays at `22:closed`; completing the full create→SSH→sync→run E2E needs an image with `sshd` preinstalled (or in-guest network egress to install it). Lifecycle create/inspect/delete is proven; the SSH-into-guest hop is the remaining gap.

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/187

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: zozo123
